chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: whenever we create numpy arrays, there are many associated properties which are really really useful

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so if we talk about the properties of numpy arrays

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: for that we create a simple array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: let's suppose we created a numpy array with this list

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: in which we have some elements

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so the most basic property which we have already talked about

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: that is array.shape

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: what we do with array.shape

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: we print the dimensions of our numpy array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so this is basically the m x n dimensions which we print

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: now because we have 1D array, so our 1D dimension is printed

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: if we wanted we could have taken a 2D array also

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so to create a 2D array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: let's create a 2D array here

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so we have a 2D array of 3 x 3 size

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: which will be created

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: along with shape, we can also print our size

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so size is basically total elements

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: and because we have an array of 3 x 3 size

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so the total elements are going to be equal to 9

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so we will have 9 printed

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: plus we can also print the data type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: if we print array.dtype

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: in this case these are going to be integer values

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so we have int64

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: in your system it might be int32 and that is completely normal

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: along with that we can also print the dimension

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: or the number of dimension values

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: we have 2 number of dimensions

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so for 2D array we have 2 printed

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: for 1D array we have 1 printed

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: for 3D array we have 3 printed

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so these are basically the total number of dimensions that we have

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: plus if we want we can convert one type of numpy array into another type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: which is called type casting

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: type casting is already in python

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so we can do type casting also

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: now type casting, we have already done an example above

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: in which we converted floating values into integer values

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so one way is that when we are creating an array from scratch

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: then we define dtype property

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: second way is if we already have an existing array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: and we want to convert its type into a new array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: let's suppose we want to make a float array from an integer value

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so what we can do for that

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: we can use a function called array.asType

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: and in this function

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: we can define the type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: np.float64

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: we can define that type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: and after that we will print the float array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: along with the float array.dtype

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so now we have this floating array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: plus its type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: that is float64

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: just like we have int64 and int128

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: similarly we have float64 and float128

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: in this way

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so we changed our type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: we have learned two ways to change type

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: if we want to change type from scratch

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: then we can change dtype property

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: or if we are creating a new array from existing array

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: then we can use asType function

chunks.title: Array
chunks.text: so these are the important properties for numpy arrays

text: whenever we create numpy arrays, there are many associated properties which are really really useful so if we talk about the properties of numpy arrays for that we create a simple array let's suppose we created a numpy array with this list in which we have some elements so the most basic property which we have already talked about that is array.shape what we do with array.shape we print the dimensions of our numpy array so this is basically the m x n dimensions which we print now because we have 1D array, so our 1D dimension is printed if we wanted we could have taken a 2D array also so to create a 2D array let's create a 2D array here 4, 5, 6 and 7, 8, 9 so we have a 2D array of 3 x 3 size which will be created along with shape, we can also print our size so size is basically total elements and because we have an array of 3 x 3 size so the total elements are going to be equal to 9 so we will have 9 printed plus we can also print the data type if we print array.dtype in this case these are going to be integer values so we have int64 in your system it might be int32 and that is completely normal along with that we can also print the dimension or the number of dimension values we have 2 number of dimensions so for 2D array we have 2 printed for 1D array we have 1 printed for 3D array we have 3 printed so these are basically the total number of dimensions that we have plus if we want we can convert one type of numpy array into another type which is called type casting type casting is already in python so we can do type casting also now type casting, we have already done an example above in which we converted floating values into integer values so one way is that when we are creating an array from scratch then we define dtype property second way is if we already have an existing array and we want to convert its type into a new array let's suppose we want to make a float array from an integer value so what we can do for that we can use a function called array.asType and in this function we can define the type np.float64 we can define that type and after that we will print the float array along with the float array.dtype so now we have this floating array plus its type that is float64 just like we have int64 and int128 similarly we have float64 and float128 in this way so we changed our type we have learned two ways to change type if we want to change type from scratch then we can change dtype property or if we are creating a new array from existing array then we can use asType function so these are the important properties for numpy arrays
